name: CloudQuery Open-Source ELT Framework
what_it_does: Open-source high-performance ELT framework with plugin architecture — enabling engineering and security teams to sync data from cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure), SaaS tools, and databases into destination data stores (PostgreSQL, BigQuery, Snowflake) using SQL for analysis, with a strong focus on cloud asset inventory and security posture management.
use_cases: Building cloud asset inventory via CloudQuery for SQL-queryable AWS/GCP/Azure resource catalogs
Syncing cloud security data via CloudQuery for CSPM and compliance reporting in SQL
Extracting SaaS data via CloudQuery plugins for GitHub, Slack, and Salesforce into warehouses
Building custom ELT pipelines via CloudQuery SDK for organization-specific data sources
Running security queries via CloudQuery SQL for CIS benchmark and compliance checks
not_for: Non-technical teams (CloudQuery requires SQL and CLI expertise)
Real-time streaming (CloudQuery is scheduled batch ELT)
Complex business transformation logic (CloudQuery extracts and loads; transforms via SQL in destination)
best_when: A DevOps or security engineering team wants to build a SQL-queryable cloud asset inventory or security posture dashboard by extracting cloud resource data into a data warehouse for analysis.
avoid_when: You need a no-code GUI, real-time streaming, or managed SaaS without CLI/infrastructure management.

auth.notes: No REST API — CloudQuery is a CLI tool / framework. CloudQuery CLI: cloudquery sync command with YAML config. Plugin authentication: each plugin uses its own auth (AWS IAM, GCP service account, GitHub token, etc.). CloudQuery Cloud: web dashboard with team auth (Email/OAuth). Plugin config: YAML-based source/destination configuration with credentials. Go SDK: for building custom plugins. gRPC: plugin protocol between CLI and plugin binaries.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open-source CLI: free; limited plugins free
pricing.paid_tiers: CloudQuery Cloud: free tier; Teams $15/user/month; Enterprise custom; Premium plugins: $0.10+/resource/month
pricing.notes: US company (San Francisco). Founded 2021 by Yevgeny Pats and Itay Zagron. $25M+ funding (a16z, Y Combinator). Apache 2.0 license for core. Plugin-based architecture: source plugins + destination plugins. 100+ plugins: AWS, GCP, Azure, GitHub, Okta, Salesforce, Cloudflare, K8s, etc. Destinations: PostgreSQL, BigQuery, Snowflake, DuckDB, S3, etc. CloudQuery Cloud: web UI for scheduling, monitoring, team management. Premium plugins: some plugins require CloudQuery Cloud subscription. CLI-first: designed for engineering teams. Security use case: cloud asset inventory + SQL for CIS/NIST/SOC2 queries.

performance.rate_limits: Source API rate limits apply per provider

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: NO REST API — CLI tool with YAML config. gRPC plugin protocol. Go SDK for custom plugins. YAML config files. Per-plugin auth (AWS/GCP/GitHub each need own credentials). Premium plugins need CloudQuery Cloud. CLI-first. Apache 2.0 core.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Sync is idempotent: same config run repeatedly produces same destination state. Append-only vs overwrite modes configurable. Incremental sync: uses state management for idempotent incremental loads.
known_agent_gotchas: NO REST API: CloudQuery is a CLI tool — automate via shell scripts or CloudQuery Cloud API
Per-plugin authentication: each source plugin needs its own credentials (AWS IAM, GCP SA, GitHub token)
Premium plugins: some plugins (e.g., Salesforce) require CloudQuery Cloud subscription
YAML config: connection and plugin config in YAML files — manage as code (GitOps-friendly)
Rate limits: each source has its own API limits (AWS: IAM-based, GCP: quota-based, etc.)
Destination deduplication: configure primary key columns to avoid duplicate records on re-sync

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced per plugin's source API. Apache 2.0: fully auditable codebase. Self-hosted: credentials stay in your environment. Per-plugin IAM: use least-privilege roles for each source (AWS read-only role, GCP viewer). Cloud asset inventory: requires broad read permissions — carefully scope IAM. YAML config: store credential references, not secrets (use environment variables or secrets manager). Go-based: memory-safe language, active CVE response. No central secret storage — credentials distributed per plugin.
